review_title: A Great Tool for Client Tracking and Retention
description: Overall, my organization found SalesForce adequate and efficient for a team of our size. It significantly increased our organization and ultimately drove client retention, providing us the most value through retargeted customers.
author: Brandon C.
pros: My favorite part about SalesForce was how detailed the tracking was. From the initial contact with a client to the final sale and every step in between, SalesForce allows you to log everything in detail.
cons: For many SalesForce has a bit of a learning curve. Any team member is able to edit certain details of tasks and customer information depending on the settings, making it intimidating for new users.

review_title: Salesforce is a solid CRM
description: It’s a good platform. I prefer it to the other one I had used at a previous employer. Overall it gets the job done.
author: Sarah L.
pros: Mostly easy to navigate and run reports. Wide range of customizations made it easy to use for sales, marketing and CS teams.
cons: The UI could be more friendly. The learning curve was rough for new employees but that went hand in hand with customizations.

review_title: Robust with a learning curve
description: Working with Salesforce was great, working within the complexities of the Salesforce framework require a ton of training. Once trained it was fairly straightforward to use.
author: Nathan B.
pros: Very robust in its integrations and community
cons: Too complex for small business or in our case, non-profit

review_title: Powerful and Flexible, but Heavy to Manage
description: My overall experience with Salesforce has been mixed. It’s an incredibly powerful and flexible platform, but at the same time, it’s demanding. I don’t always enjoy using it day to day, but I recognize that in the right hands and with the right resources, it’s one of the most capable CRM platforms available.
author: Gabriel K.
pros: Salesforce is incredibly powerful and flexible. If you need a CRM that can scale with your organization, handle complex data relationships, and adapt to highly customized workflows, it’s hard to beat. The ecosystem is massive, integrations are strong, and once things are configured correctly, it can support very sophisticated reporting, automation, and segmentation across teams. From a marketing and communications perspective, Salesforce shines when it comes to data depth. When structured well, it enables meaningful insights into audiences, journeys, and long-term engagement that simpler tools just can’t provide.
cons: Salesforce is not intuitive, and it is not lightweight. The learning curve is steep, especially for users who are adjacent to admin work rather than full-time CRM specialists. Many tasks that feel like they should be simple require significant setup, technical knowledge, or ongoing maintenance. To get the most out of Salesforce, you need real investment—either in staff expertise, consultants, or both. Without that, it’s easy for the system to feel overwhelming, slow, or underutilized. The power is there, but it comes at the cost of time, complexity, and cognitive load.

review_title: Really Loved Using Salesforce
description: Overall, I think Salesforce is a great system to use either as your AMS or CDP. The integration was smooth and straightforward. Training could be accomplished very easily and was mostly intuitive. You can't say this about a lot of systems. I also appreciated the amount of time our account rep spent with our group to ensure all of our training needs were met. I would definitely recommend this system to others or at least advise that it be one of your top runners for consideration of a new system.
author: Tina E.
pros: Salesforce was a great system to use in terms of having a customer data platform. We used it when we had a legacy system that originated in the 1980's, (yes, really) but were not yet ready to take the plunge to get a new AMS. What I loved about using salesforce was that we could see a lot of information about the customer in one place. The system was very easy to navigate. We were able to track customer activity without having to check back with the legacy system. We were also able to assist customers very easily with items such as account updates and reset. I didn't really have any complaints about using salesforce.
cons: One of the main reasons our service team used salesforce was to track customer contacts. The system was good about noting receipt of the contacts once an email was received. It was pretty instantaneous. What I didn't like, or probably had not learned, was how to integrate auto-assign by message types. Instead, I had to rely on my team actively taking ownership of customer tickets, which doesn't usually happen.

review_title: Marketing and Sales Enablement Champs
description: Salesforce is an incredible tool for marketing managers who want to create and monitor campaigns to enable salespeople to work out deals.
author: Logan B.
pros: I love utilizing the campaigns and reporting features for marketing to understand attribution and ROI for various different marketing campaigns. The ability for sales to log and schedule calls and emails to leads and customers is another plus.
cons: There was functionality in Salesforce Classic that was lost in upgrading to Salesforce Lightning; however, most of that was resolved and there is a help doc showing how to utilize the new systems.

review_title: Salesforce Platform Super powerful for customer relationship, customizable, system workflows, dashboards and automations for organization.
description: My overall experience with Salesforce Platform has been very positive. It’s a robust, flexible system that can transform how teams manage customer relationships, automate processes, and analyze data. Once you get past the initial learning curve, it becomes an incredibly valuable tool that supports efficiency and growth
author: Dionne Joy M.
pros: What I liked most about Salesforce Platform, great ability to bring everything together in one place. Love the task managing, customer support and provides value for money. Its biggest strength is customization, code updates, development and code management. You can tailor almost every part of the system — workflows, dashboards, automations, and even entire apps, without needing deep technical skills. and provides great security.
cons: What I liked Least about Salesforce Platform is it can be a little complex and require lots of learning for users. Salesforce Platform is powerful, but that power comes with complexity. The interface can feel overwhelming at first, especially for new users. There’s also a steep learning curve and mastering the platform often requires training or certification. Another drawback is cost.

review_title: The app businesses can depend on!
description: I appreciate its versatility in handling various business needs, along with robust cloud services that enhance collaboration and efficiency. Its strong development for community support and regular updates further contribute to a positive user experience.
author: JOIA W.
pros: The Salesforce Platform is highly valued for its user-friendly interface, extensive customization options, and integration capabilities. It's Great honestly, seems very secure and makes task management smooth.
cons: I noticed it can be considered expensive, especially for small businesses or organizations that need multiple licenses or advanced features.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great overall product for client management
description: Overall the salesforce platform is a great tool. I have used other platforms with previous employers but this one works much smoother and efficient.
author: Tyler L.
pros: Easy to use product with great navigation. Makes jobs easier for Frontline employees to access client information and provide fast efficient response.
cons: Product does have a slightly slower load time on mobile platforms. Navigation could be improved with a more intuitive interface on these devices.

review_title: Five Stars and A Thumbs Up
description: Using Salesforce to multitask while on a call was overall the best customer relations management app.
author: Kenidra S.
pros: I was able to locate customers when calls came through. The whole system was very easy to use when operating it.
cons: Somes a call would take place and a person's information didn't populate so I had to input it manually.

review_title: Selected platform for our business processes
description: We decided upon a platform approach for digitizing our business processes. We went though a significant procurement exercise to identify the most appropriate tool based upon our business requirements and salesforce was the decided solution. We initially implemented on one key process within the organisation and then have prioritized and adopted other key business processes as we mature using the system.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Our approach means that we have all key business processes (and subsequent data) in one location. This also provided consistency in approach and allows us to transform our processes as we adopt onto Salesforce. The tool itself is incredibly flexible and powerful, and has never failed to meet our needs.
cons: Development / developers need to be expert in Salesforce code, a non-technical user is not able to configure the system, even with 'easy' requirements. It's not a massive issue BUT one that small/medium size organisations need to plan for.

review_title: Salesforce is ok
description: Its been a hard road and many fixes but it basically does what it is supposed to. Too much money to have to add to a system that should already work.
author: Amy W.
pros: Has many functions available to use. Everything combined can be used for your invocing and accounting systems.
cons: Have to go multiple places and fix multiple things for salesforce to work for you. For example to print an invoice you have to go to four different places just to print. Every time it updates, it drops something else that was working.

review_title: Convenience achieved with Salesforce!
description: I've used Salesforce at four different companies, in three different industries and three different roles. It has always been a top notch platform to use!
author: Richelle H.
pros: Salesforce makes staying on track with client follow up extremely efficient and seamless! It's also easily trackable! I also love that just any user can't manipulate it and Salesforce truly tracks when things are done in the system, down to the exact time!
cons: I personally don't see any cons with Salesforce. However, when I've seen Salespeople lie about their follow up, Salesforce will bring them out of the dark and into the light!!!

review_title: A great software that has it all in one
description: Overall I enjoy using Salesforce, it has the capability of providing a great way to manage customer data for researching accounts while allowing you to add notes and process payments quickly.
author: Heather R.
pros: It’s easy to not only process payments but see the entire account payment history. It helps reduce the time I spend on looking into customer accounts by having to use only one software . It gives in-depth customer insights enabling me to customize offers effectively.
cons: Salesforce can be challenging to navigate due to its layout and the lack of a visually appealing interface. This can make it difficult to quickly access information as it’s hard to see with the current format.

review_title: Salesforce A+
description: Overall positive, I would recommend this product to others if asked. It's ease of use is great.
author: John R.
pros: Ease of use, great search function, quick load times
cons: Inability to close all tabs open in SF with one click.

review_title: Salesforce - easy to navigate, some data you need another app
description: Good, easy to use platform, some improvements needed.
author: Nynke S.
pros: Easy to navigate and use. A program we use daily to help us process orders, pipeline cleaning etc.
cons: Some data hard to find, external programs needed.

review_title: The absolute best - and you pay for it
description: We were able to utilize SF to manage our CRM, setup drip funnel campaigns, ebb and flow of lead from start to close (and track every person that touched the lead for evaluation purposes).
author: Jonathan G.
pros: Completely customizable to the company/business needs. Incredibly powerful tech to automate nearly every marketing channel in a large scale business. Once its built out, you'll never need another marketing tech product.
cons: Very robust and is priced as such. Not a great solution for small-to-medium sized businesses.

review_title: Paralegal Assistant
author: Olivia B.
pros: I like that you are able to track communication with numerous providers by using salesforce. It is a one stop shop.
cons: When you upload a document that is either missing information, you are required to resubmit the entire request.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: All in one data capture and collaboration tool
description: All in one data capture tool to collaborate between teams and regions in an organization.
author: Rakesh T.
pros: Easy to view, filter cases and update accordingly. Automatically sends email when changes are made. Can track inventory, issues, cases, business opportunities all in one place.
cons: Heavy platform for some simple tasks. Also would be better if layout is simplified.

review_title: Amy's review
author: Amy M.
pros: Salesforce is great! it is very beneficial and provides different services.
cons: Nothing. It is easy to navigate and has a lot of features.

review_title: Great platform for information sharing
description: Great resource for compiling and sharing information
author: Jun H.
pros: Outlook plug in was one of the best features by far, live updates instead of having to refresh browser
cons: navigating the tabs and pages (individually having to close each tab)

review_title: Salesforce
author: Adrianna T.
pros: The variety and customization available for a complete CRM experience.
cons: The integration with other apps can get boggy.

review_title: Salesforce Platform
description: it is a great platform that allows you to enter and track sale related information.
author: Faraz M.
pros: I like that there are different features that help track what is sold and targets
cons: I dislike the layout of the software and think it should be more accessibile or have the option for these features

review_title: The Ultimate CRM and Business Solution
author: Manon S.
pros: I like most about Salesforce is its powerful CRM capabilities. I can effortlessly track and manage customer interactions in one place.
cons: It can be overwhelming with its numerous features. Navigating through all the options can be confusing for a new user.

review_title: Excellent Enterprise Platform for nearly all industries with a Enterprise-sized Price Tag
author: Chris F.
pros: I love how they have turned a database program into an interactive place where companies can create a singular source of truth surrounding every aspect of business operations.
cons: Some limitations are common sense additions that have been neglected in favor of flashy, new features. Reporting is limited and has many issues, one of which is they barely support it currently with little/no improvements while pushing users towards their Analytics Add-On - which has many improvements that were requested on Reports.
